---
title: "Food Product Quality Report"
subtitle: "Nutrition analysis of Open Food Facts products"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  eval: false
---

```{python}
import duckdb
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

con = duckdb.connect("../food.db", read_only=True)
```

## Nutrition Summary by Grade

```{python}
summary = con.execute("SELECT * FROM nutrition_summary ORDER BY nutriscore_grade").fetchdf()
summary
```

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(12, 4))

summary.plot.bar(x="nutriscore_grade", y="avg_kcal", ax=axes[0], legend=False, color="#2196F3")
axes[0].set_title("Avg Calories (kcal/100g)")
axes[0].set_xlabel("")

summary.plot.bar(x="nutriscore_grade", y="avg_sugar", ax=axes[1], legend=False, color="#FF9800")
axes[1].set_title("Avg Sugar (g/100g)")
axes[1].set_xlabel("")

summary.plot.bar(x="nutriscore_grade", y="avg_protein", ax=axes[2], legend=False, color="#4CAF50")
axes[2].set_title("Avg Protein (g/100g)")
axes[2].set_xlabel("")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Suspicious Products

Products where the Nutri-Score grade seems inconsistent with the nutrition numbers.

```{python}
# TODO: uncomment after completing suspicious_products.sql
# suspicious = con.execute("SELECT * FROM suspicious_products LIMIT 20").fetchdf()
# suspicious
```

## Model Performance

```{python}
# TODO: load confusion matrix and classification report
# after running train_model.py and saving results
```

## Similar Products

```{python}
# TODO: pick a product and show 5 nearest neighbours by embedding
# after running the embedding pipeline
```

```{python}
con.close()
```
